name: Tee It Up Indoor-Indoor Golf Simulator
about: Tee It Up Indoor is a private indoor golf simulator venue located in Clearfield, UT. It offers 24/7/365 access to guests via remote control from their smartphones, making it a convenient option for play at any time. The facility utilizes Foresight GCQuad technology, a premium launch monitor, and provides access to a variety of iconic golf courses. It operates on a pay-by-the-hour model with options for punch passes and memberships, targeting both serious golfers looking for practice and those seeking a casual, fun golf experience.
why_golfers_like_it: 24/7 access via smartphone for maximum convenience
Premium Foresight GCQuad launch monitor for highly accurate data tracking
Completely private, self-serve experience for a personalized atmosphere

food_and_drink.notes: BYOB allowed. Venue also has a fully stocked fridge with soda and water, and a variety of snacks for purchase. Each session includes one premium snack and drink.
BYOB allowed. Guests can bring their own alcoholic drinks.

simulator_hardware.notes: The game's most advanced launch monitor, using advanced imaging technology and a lightning-fast processor.

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: No, a right-handed adult club set is available for free use. However, you are encouraged to bring your own clubs, and all clubs must be clean before use.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: Absolutely, you are more than welcome to bring your own food and drinks. The venue also has a fully stocked fridge with soda, water, and snacks for purchase.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: It depends on skill level, but on average, one golfer can get through 18 holes in about 1 hour on the simulator. Twosomes can usually finish in less than 2 hours.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes. The venue features carnival-style games like putt skee, balloon pop, and darts that are designed to be fun for the entire family, including beginning golfers.
